search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae gastroenteritis adults Issue 2 diarrhea history red flags investigations referral
candidate_sources_rejected: fluid, antimicrobial, antidiarrheal and admission recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Diarrhea has infectious and non-infectious causes.
No treatment, medicine, dose, testing, admission or discharge instruction was mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with acute diarrhea; children, pregnancy, chronic diarrhea, inflammatory bowel disease, immunocompromise and recent surgery require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: DHA virtual-care documentation domains partially transfer to UAE emergency assessment.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA evidence; UAE infection-control, public-health, laboratory, antimicrobial and emergency pathways apply.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 (2024) exact source was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No later official DHA issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA adult gastroenteritis history section supports onset, duration, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and risk context for adult diarrhea without diagnosing gastroenteritis.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports measured vital data, general appearance and time-critical symptoms only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording existing test results reviewed by the clinician without recommending testing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or handover only when already decided or arranged.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The adult virtual-care scope was reviewed; pediatric, chronic, inflammatory, surgical and immunocompromised presentations remain outside direct scope.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines adult gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

unresolved_source_gaps: Frequency, volume, blood or mucus, vomiting, hydration, travel, exposure, outbreak, medicine and antibiotic history, abdominal examination, diagnosis, stool testing, treatment and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
